perturbation.original_text: 1.16. "Minimum Payment Period" has the meaning set forth on attached Attachment B. 3 *** Portions of this page have been omitted pursuant to a request for Confidential Treatment filed separately with the Commission.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.16. "Minimum Payment Period" has the meaning set forth on attached Attachment B and shall be interpreted as calendar months. 3 *** Portions of this page have been omitted pursuant to a request for Confidential Treatment filed separately with the Commission.
perturbation.explanation: The added text, interpreting 'Minimum Payment Period' as calendar months, contradicts Section 2.4, which defines the Minimum Payment Periods in terms of one-year periods. This introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of each payment period, creating ambiguity in the contract and uncertainty in enforcement. Is it calendar months or years?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4. Minimum Payments. Integra shall use commercially reasonable efforts to Sell Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products that generate payments to PcoMed of no less than the Minimum Payments applicable to each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment applicable to each Minimum Payment Period shall be due annually on or before 45 days after the last day of each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment may be satisfied either by payments of the Fees paid pursuant to Sections 2.2 and 2.3, or by the sum of Fees paid and an additional elective cash payment from Integra to PcoMed. It shall remain in Integra's sole discretion whether or not to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period by making an additional elective cash payment. In the event that Integra fails to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period, PcoMed may, at its sole election, give notice, as set forth in Section 3.2, for conversion of Integra's exclusive arrangement under Section 3.1 to a non-exclusive arrangement. PcoMed's conversion right is PcoMed's sole and exclusive remedy for Integra's failure to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period. Integra shall have no liability at any time to PcoMed for Integra's failure to pay the Minimum Payment.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies the definition of 'Minimum Payment Period' to be interpreted as 'calendar months'. This directly contradicts Section 2.4, which states that 'Minimum Payments' are applicable to each 'Minimum Payment Period' and are 'due annually'. By defining the period as calendar months in 1.16, it creates a direct conflict with the annual timeframe established in 2.4. This is a strong in-text contradiction because it creates ambiguity about whether the minimum payment is calculated monthly or annually, leading to potential disputes and impacting enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4. Minimum Payments. Integra shall use commercially reasonable efforts to Sell Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products that generate payments to PcoMed of no less than the Minimum Payments applicable to each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment applicable to each Minimum Payment Period shall be due annually on or before 45 days after the last day of each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment may be satisfied either by payments of the Fees paid pursuant to Sections 2.2 and 2.3, or by the sum of Fees paid and an additional elective cash payment from Integra to PcoMed. It shall remain in Integra's sole discretion whether or not to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period by making an additional elective cash payment. In the event that Integra fails to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period, PcoMed may, at its sole election, give notice, as set forth in Section 3.2, for conversion of Integra's exclusive arrangement under Section 3.1 to a non-exclusive arrangement. PcoMed's conversion right is PcoMed's sole and exclusive remedy for Integra's failure to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period. Integra shall have no liability at any time to PcoMed for Integra's failure to pay the Minimum Payment.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4. Minimum Payments. Integra shall use commercially reasonable efforts to Sell Treated Integra Products and Partially Treated Integra Products that generate payments to PcoMed of no less than the Minimum Payments applicable to each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment applicable to each Minimum Payment Period shall be due annually on or before 45 days after the last day of each Minimum Payment Period. The Minimum Payment may be satisfied either by payments of the Fees paid pursuant to Sections 2.2 and 2.3, or by the sum of Fees paid and an additional elective cash payment from Integra to PcoMed. It shall remain in Integra's sole discretion whether or not to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period by making an additional elective cash payment. In the event that Integra fails to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period, PcoMed may, at its sole election, give notice, as set forth in Section 3.2, for conversion of Integra's exclusive arrangement under Section 3.1 to a non-exclusive arrangement. However, PcoMed can not convert the exclusive arrangement, if Integra's failure to pay is due to circumstances outside of their control. PcoMed's conversion right is PcoMed's sole and exclusive remedy for Integra's failure to satisfy the Minimum Payment for any Minimum Payment Period. Integra shall have no liability at any time to PcoMed for Integra's failure to pay the Minimum Payment.
perturbation.explanation: The added text restricts PcoMed's right to convert the agreement to a non-exclusive arrangement if Integra's failure to pay is due to circumstances outside their control. However, this contradicts the preceding sentences, which state that PcoMed, at its sole election, may give notice for conversion upon Integra's failure to satisfy the minimum payment. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding PcoMed's rights upon Integra's failure to pay. It's unclear whether PcoMed can convert or if there are exceptions, causing confusion in enforcement. What circumstances are outside the party's control?
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2. Conversion of Rights to Non-Exclusive. Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 3.1, if Integra (i) fails to timely pay any Minimum Payments due under Section 2.4 for any Minimum Payment Period or (ii) fails to make the payments described in Sections 2.1, 2.2, or 2.3 when due, or otherwise defaults under any provision of this Agreement, the exclusive Rights granted to Integra under Section 3.1 shall, at the option of PcoMed, to be exercised in PcoMed's sole and absolute discretion at any time, convert to a non-exclusive arrangement provided that PcoMed gives Integra written notice of its breach and Integra does not cure such breach within forty-five (45) days following Integra's receipt of such notice. If PcoMed makes such election, the Right shall be a nonexclusive right at the end of such 45-day cure period, and PcoMed may thereafter allow other Third Parties to use the PcoMed Surface Modification Technology in products that are in competition with the Integra Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a condition where PcoMed cannot convert the agreement to non-exclusive if Integra's failure to pay is due to circumstances outside of their control. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (3.2) which states that if Integra fails to pay minimum payments, the exclusive rights granted to Integra under Section 3.1 *shall*, at the option of PcoMed, convert to a non-exclusive arrangement. The added condition introduces uncertainty and contradicts PcoMed's right to convert the agreement, leading to an inconsistency in enforcement. This fits the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction".

perturbation.original_text: 6.1. Payment Terms and Reports. Payments due under Section 2.2 shall be payable to PcoMed by Integra on a quarterly basis within 45 days following the end of each calendar quarter. Each such payment shall be accompanied by a statement setting forth in reasonable detail (i) the number and type of Treated Integra Product and Partially Treated Integra Product sold and the Net Sales applicable thereto, (ii) a breakdown of all the components of Net Sales for the determination of payments due under Sections 2.2 (the numbers may be and type of products may be stated in the aggregate and not by customer and are not required to be detailed by geographic area unless Fee rates are different in different geographic areas). Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product shall be considered as being sold for the purpose of the calculation of payments due under Sections 2.2 when the payments for the Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product are received by Integra or its Affiliates from a Third Party. All payments to be made under this Agreement shall be paid in United States dollars. Net Sales of Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product and fees in currencies other than United States dollars shall be first determined in the currency of the country in which they are earned and shall be converted (for the purpose of calculation only) in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles for financial reporting in the United States.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1. Payment Terms and Reports. Payments due under Section 2.2 shall be payable to PcoMed by Integra on a quarterly basis within 45 days following the end of each calendar quarter, unless otherwise agreed in a separate written agreement. Each such payment shall be accompanied by a statement setting forth in reasonable detail (i) the number and type of Treated Integra Product and Partially Treated Integra Product sold and the Net Sales applicable thereto, (ii) a breakdown of all the components of Net Sales for the determination of payments due under Sections 2.2 (the numbers may be and type of products may be stated in the aggregate and not by customer and are not required to be detailed by geographic area unless Fee rates are different in different geographic areas). Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product shall be considered as being sold for the purpose of the calculation of payments due under Sections 2.2 when the payments for the Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product are received by Integra or its Affiliates from a Third Party. All payments to be made under this Agreement shall be paid in United States dollars. Net Sales of Treated Integra Product and/or Partially Treated Integra Product and fees in currencies other than United States dollars shall be first determined in the currency of the country in which they are earned and shall be converted (for the purpose of calculation only) in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles for financial reporting in the United States.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "unless otherwise agreed in a separate written agreement," the initial clause regarding payment terms becomes contradictory. The original clause sets a definite payment deadline (45 days after the end of each calendar quarter), but the altered clause suggests this deadline can be modified through a separate agreement. This creates in-text contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement, as it's unclear whether the initial deadline is binding. This contradicts Section 12.7 (Modification) that says "No change, modification, addition or amendment to this Agreement is valid or enforceable unless in writing and signed and dated by the authorized officers of the parties to this Agreement."
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.7. Modification. No change, modification, addition or amendment to this Agreement is valid or enforceable unless in writing and signed and dated by the authorized officers of the parties to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 6.1 introduces an exception ('unless otherwise agreed in a separate written agreement') to the payment terms, which directly contradicts Section 12.7's requirement that any modification to the agreement must be in writing and signed by authorized officers of both parties. The change creates uncertainty regarding the enforceability of the original payment terms and the formal modification process, thus establishing a strong in-text contradiction, per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
